candidates.title: 11 teens charged after Montreal police investigates moments prior to police killing Longueuil teen
candidates.description: On Tuesday, Montreal police announced that 11 suspects are facing criminal charges following a Montreal police investigation into the moments leading to the killing of 15-year-old Nooran Rezayi. The suspects were between 13 and 17 years old at time of the shooting.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Jasper, Alta., to get $520M in additional reconstruction funding from federal government after 2024 wildfire
candidates.description: Jasper will receive more than half a billion dollars in funding from the federal government to accelerate the rebuilding of destroyed and damaged park assets and provide temporary housing for residents displaced by the 2024 wildfire.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: State of emergency, flash flood warning issued in southwestern Manitoba after deluge
candidates.description: Southwestern Manitoba is under a flash flood warning and the Municipality of Boissevain-Morton has declared a state of emergency after torrential rains hit the storm-battered region again on Monday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Glacial lake near Pemberton, B.C., bursts, sending flood water to small community
candidates.description: Experts believe flood waters are likely to peak late in the day on Wednesday and people are being asked to stay away from Gates Lake and nearby creeks due to dangerous conditions.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Heat dome brings dangerous temperatures. Here's how to stay safe
candidates.description: As parts of southern Ontario, Quebec and the eastern United States swelter through a heat dome, here are the health warning signs to watch out for — and how to keep cool.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ottawa-Gatineau under thunderstorm watch, tornado warning ended for Kingston
candidates.description: Environment Canada has issued a severe thunderstorm watch for the Ottawa-Gatineau area. The weather agency has rescinded an earlier tornado warning for the Kingston area.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 6 people presumed drowned as RCMP lead recovery of sunken boat near Richmond, B.C.
candidates.description: An RCMP underwater recovery team is now searching for a capsized vessel that sank off the shores of Richmond, B.C., on Sunday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Fort Simpson spared the worst overnight after wildfire stopped on edge of town
candidates.description: A shift in winds overnight prevented the vast, out-of-control wildfire that forced the evacuation of the community from spreading to structures in town.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: What goes into making World Cup-quality turf, and the Canadian behind it
candidates.description: When Australian goalkeeper Patrick Beach came off the turf after the first FIFA World Cup game in Vancouver, it wasn't the crowd or atmosphere that had caught his attention — it was the grass.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: More families turning to financial aid to afford overnight summer camps
candidates.description: The rising cost of living is forcing many Canadian families to make tough choices about whether they can afford to send their kids away for camp.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Carney promises Arctic investment will benefit Inuit during meeting with leadership
candidates.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is meeting with Inuit leadership Tuesday in Kuujjuaq, the largest community in the Nunavik region in northern Quebec. It's the first trip by a sitting prime minister to the village in almost 20 years.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Judiciary must listen to ordinary people, says Supreme Court nominee Glenn Joyal
candidates.description: Canada's newest Supreme Court nominee believes that better outreach and communication of the work of the judiciary can restore trust in the institution at a time when judges are under increasing scrutiny.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Petition signature counting resumes as Alberta separatist group gets partial win at court of appeal
candidates.description: An Alberta separatist group has secured a partial win at the province’s top court after a lower court judge quashed its referendum petition last month.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Hope fades of finding more Venezuela earthquake survivors as official death toll rises
candidates.description: Rescue teams in Venezuela were losing hope on Tuesday of finding more survivors of twin earthquakes that struck the country last week, following hours of exhausting work searching for victims beneath the rubble of collapsed buildings.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: South Africa's anti-migrant protesters march nationwide after thousands flee violence
candidates.description: Anti-immigrant protesters draped in flags and wielding wooden weapons marched across cities in South Africa on Tuesday to mark a deadline they had set for undocumented migrants to leave, with some marches hit by violence and looting.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: NDAs, permits and street closures: Evidence is mounting for a Taylor Swift wedding at Madison Square Garden
candidates.description: Ever since Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce announced their engagement, fans have been dying for details. While absolutely nothing is confirmed by the couple themselves, here's what's got people talking.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ukrainian-born oligarch with Russian ties reportedly injured in Monaco explosion
candidates.description: Police in Monaco and France were searching for a ‌suspected bomber on Tuesday after three people were wounded by an explosion in the wealthy principality, which two sources said was an attack on a Ukrainian-born oligarch.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: IN PHOTOS | See the stunning strawberry moon
candidates.description: The so-called strawberry moon, which marks the first full moon of the summer, was on full display last night.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Venezuelans race against clock looking for earthquake survivors
candidates.description: Five days have passed since twin earthquakes struck northern Venezuela, killing more than 1,450 people, according to the government. Relief organizations say the first 72 hours after a natural disaster is the most crucial time period for rescues, though survival can be extended if people have access to food and water.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Russian, Belarusian figure skaters can return to international events with approved neutral status
candidates.description: Russian skaters can return to world championships and Grand Prix events next season as neutral athletes for the first time since the full military invasion of Ukraine.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump's death spiral of threatening Iran | About That
candidates.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has once again threatened to wipe out Iran, saying 'the Islamic Republic of Iran will no longer exist' if Iran continues to violate the ceasefire agreement. Andrew Chang breaks down the cycle of the two countries' recent attacks, retaliations and perceived provocations to try to understand how they keep vacillating between conflict and dealmaking. (CLARIFICATION: June 30, 2026): The title shown at the beginning of this video should read: 'Trump's death spiral of threatening Iran'.) (Photo credits: The Canadian Press, Reuters, Adobe Stock and Getty Images)
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Sudan declares new cholera outbreak months after official end to one that lasted nearly 2 years
candidates.description: Sudan has declared a new outbreak of cholera, the country's Ministry of Heath reported Monday, with 117 deaths in West Kordofan state, another seven confirmed cases and 838 suspected cases.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Some Venezuelans fear a dark and dangerous future in aftermath of twin earthquakes
candidates.description: The future seems dark and dangerous for some Venezuelans amid the dust, debris and death shrouding parts of the country that suffered the brunt of twin earthquakes, creating one of the worst natural disasters in its history.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Italy, Balkans latest in Europe to feel brunt of record-breaking heat wave
candidates.description: Italy and the Balkans felt the impact on Monday of a record-breaking heat wave that has caused hundreds of excess deaths and disrupted daily life across the continent for more than a week, with growing concerns over the ‌spread of wildfires.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Inside the World Cup's international police command centre
candidates.description: CBC News got rare access inside the International Police Cooperation Center in Virginia, where law enforcement agencies from around the world are coordinating security for the FIFA World Cup, including monitoring drone threats and sharing intelligence.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: U.S. Supreme Court rejects Trump-led challenge against mail-in ballots
candidates.description: The U.S. Supreme Court on Monday ruled that states can count ballots that arrive after election day, a persistent target of President Donald Trump.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: As winds of change blow toward Cuba, how will Havana chart a path forward?
candidates.description: Six months into a tumultuous year for Cuba, the winds of change are aggressively blowing across the Straits of Florida, toward the island nation.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: U.S. Supreme Court declines Trump's appeal in E. Jean Carroll case
candidates.description: The U.S. Supreme Court declined on Monday to hear President Donald Trump's bid to overturn a $5-million US verdict in favor of E. Jean Carroll.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: U.S. Supreme Court rules against Trump bid to fire Lisa Cook, Federal Reserve governor
candidates.description: The U.S. Supreme Court refused on Monday ​to let Donald Trump fire Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook as it stood firm to preserve the central bank's cherished independence against an unprecedented challenge by the Republican president.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada's economy reverses months of slow growth with 0.5% boost in April
candidates.description: Canada's economy showed signs of rebounding by growing 0.5 per cent in April, reversing months of slow and negative economic growth. Real gross domestic product is up half a percentage point largely because of growth in the mining, quarrying, and oil and gas extraction sectors, Statistics Canada said in its latest report, released Tuesday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Tariff deal with Trump unlikely before U.S. midterms, says Canada's former trade chief
candidates.description: Canada's former chief trade negotiator says he doesn't expect Ottawa to reach a tariff deal with Washington before the U.S. midterm elections.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: WhatsApp will allow users to go by usernames, closing a privacy blind spot
candidates.description: WhatsApp users will soon get the option of going by usernames instead of phone numbers, the company said Monday, announcing plans to address a privacy blind spot. WhatsApp, which says it has more than 3 billion users globally, has until now allowed users to be contacted by anyone who has their phone number.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: What are US and Japanese soldiers doing in the middle of the Australian bush?
candidates.description: The BBC's Katy Watson explains why troops are training in remote Australia - a country not at war, thousands of kilometres from today's major conflicts.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'Absolute madness': Row over plan to demolish Nazi bunker under Berlin
candidates.description: City officials want to build flats on the city centre site but others say it should be preserved as part of Germany's history.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Manhunt after bomb injures Ukrainian oligarch in Monaco
candidates.description: The blast was caused by an explosive device which appeared to contain bolts and pellets, the head of Monaco's government said.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: US envoys in Doha to meet mediators but not Iranians, Qatar says
candidates.description: Qatar's foreign ministry spokesman says no high-level meetings or direct talks between the US and Iran are scheduled.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Missing US congressman explains mysterious four-month absence
candidates.description: New Jersey Republican Tom Kean Jr, told fellow lawmakers that depression was the reason he had been away since March.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Founder of Asian super-app Gojek sentenced to years in jail for corruption
candidates.description: The case against Gojek founder Nadiem Makarim relates to a laptop deal made during his term as Indonesia's education minister.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Flooding hits Ghana's capital killing 13 people - with another storm forecast
candidates.description: People have been urged to relocate to high ground or stay indoors as more rain is expected to come.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: One big win and three defeats for Trump in dramatic day at Supreme Court
candidates.description: While Trump celebrated a ruling expanding presidential power to remove and replace regulators, other decisions were major setbacks.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Six people shot dead at centre for mothers and children in Germany
candidates.description: The male suspect who has been arrested was in a custody dispute over his baby daughter, police say.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Lilo & Stitch star Daveigh Chase's cause of death was Aids
candidates.description: The actress, who also crawled out of a TV in horror film The Ring, passed away in LA this month, aged 35.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Supreme Court blocks Trump's attempt to fire Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook
candidates.description: The decision, seen as a win for central bank independence, sends the fight over removal back to the lower courts.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'You are Canadian heroes' - the forgotten hosts making World Cup history
candidates.description: Canada are making history at this World Cup with a run that is likely to change the face of football in the country forever.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Teyana Taylor's tears and Lauryn Hill tribute steal show at BET Awards
candidates.description: Taylor had an emotional reaction to being presented with the Icon of the Year award by Janet Jackson.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Aftershock frays nerves as many Venezuelans left to fend for themselves
candidates.description: In areas devastated by the twin earthquakes, people are using crowbars, pickaxes and their bare hands to try to reach survivors.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: What to know about the looming deadline on North American free trade
candidates.description: The US, Canada and Mexico were to reach an agreement on whether to extend their free trade pact by 1 July. All signs point to them blowing past that deadline.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'No one move!': The agonising silence as Venezuela rescuers listen for survivors
candidates.description: With tens of thousands of people still believed missing, teams listen for signs of life beneath the rubble.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: How population decline is exposing Germany's old divides
candidates.description: Decades after the fall of the Berlin Wall, a population slump in the east lays bare the existing divisions
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Watch: Young bald eagle takes first flight in California
candidates.description: A popular stream set up by the Friends of Big Bear Valley caught the moment a juvenile eagle nicknamed Luna spread its wings.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Rescuers keep up search for survivors at collapsed La Guaira building
candidates.description: At least 1,700 people are known to have been killed in the disaster but hope remains that others can continue to be found alive.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: What do we know about the shooting in Stade, Germany?
candidates.description: The BBC's Jessica Parker reports from the scene of the shooting that killed six people in northern Germany.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Potentially dangerous heatwave to sweep across the eastern United States
candidates.description: A heatwave developing across the eastern United States will bring daytime heat, high humidity and stifling overnight temperatures as Ben Rich explains.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Keep an eye out for tonight's Strawberry Moon
candidates.description: The Strawberry Moon is set to rise in UK skies tonight, marking the first full Moon of the summer season. It will reach its peak in the early hours of Tuesday, 30 June, according to the Royal Observatory Greenwich.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: First dinosaur bone from Antarctica found in a drawer
candidates.description: The fossil, collected in Antarctica in 1985, is part of the tail of a beast called a Titanosaur.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Where is best to see the Strawberry Moon?
candidates.description: The first full Moon of the summer will be visible tonight, reaching its peak in the early hours of Tuesday.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: SCOTUS to rule on birthright citizenship. And, U.S. murder rate nears new low
candidates.description: The Supreme Court is expected to make a long-awaited ruling on birthright citizenship today, on the high court's last day of its term. And, the U.S. murder rate approaches a record low.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Venezuelans deported from the U.S. were killed hours later in powerful quakes
candidates.description: On June 24, 146 Venezuelans were deported from Texas to Caracas. Hours later, while the deportees were in a guarded hotel, powerful twin earthquakes struck.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Iran war supercharges pivot to renewable energy
candidates.description: The Iran war and high oil and gas prices have supercharged the adoption of renewables and EVs worldwide. Global investors say these technologies make financial sense and increase energy security.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Explosion in Monaco injures 3, including Ukrainian tycoon
candidates.description: Monaco 's chief prosecutor said the suspect who placed an explosive device that injured three people, including a reported Ukrainian tycoon, acted alone and remains at large.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Chinese billionaire Guo Wengui gets 30 years in U.S. prison for fraud conviction
candidates.description: Guo said he came to the U.S. to destroy the Chinese Communist Party. But the judge said he instead diverted investor money to live lavishly.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Venezuela's deadly quakes put its U.S.-backed government to the test
candidates.description: Venezuela's La Guaira state bore the brunt of the earthquake damage, bringing memories of a 1999 disaster that became President Hugo Chávez's first major test. Now, it's the acting leader's challenge.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Pakistani airstrikes kill 36 civilians in Afghanistan and wound 160, officials say
candidates.description: Pakistan says it targeted militant hideouts along the Pakistan-Afghanistan border in response to militant attacks across Pakistan. Afghanistan condemned the strikes as acts of aggression.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: U.S. and Iran exchange fire despite ceasefire. And, Trump nominates a new head of ICE
candidates.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged strikes over the weekend, putting their latest ceasefire in jeopardy. And, Trump has nominated a former Oklahoma state trooper to head Immigration and Customs Enforcement.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Trump says the U.S. and Iran will meet in Qatar after weekend attacks
candidates.description: President Trump said talks with Iran would resume Tuesday in Qatar, despite the two sides trading attacks in the Gulf over the weekend. Iran did not confirm whether it will participate.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Former Iran nuclear deal negotiator on what U.S.-Iran 'new normal' looks like
candidates.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks former national security adviser and 2015 Iran deal negotiator Jake Sullivan about ceasefire talks amid new strikes by the U.S. and Iran.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Tricks to help you spend less money at restaurants
candidates.description: Personal finance and nutrition experts share simple strategies that make it possible to eat out without spending a fortune. One tip? You might have to let go of your fast food delivery habit.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Computer scientist on why he believes mass layoffs due to AI is a 'convenient excuse'
candidates.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Princeton computer scientist Sayash Kapoor about his assertions that AI won't lead to mass layoffs.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Supreme Court cements Trump's power over agencies long considered independent
candidates.description: In a 6-3 decision, the Supreme Court struck down a 91-year-old precedent that has prevented presidents from removing members of independent agencies meant to be a check on his power.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Red, white and glowing blue: Trump's push for new reactors reaches the finish line
candidates.description: A program initiated by the Trump administration has allowed small companies to rush their testing of several new nuclear reactor designs. Some worry that safety is being compromised.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: FDA scientists flag concerns with peptides, the trendy molecules RFK Jr. supports
candidates.description: Documents reviewing the research on peptides note lack of good evidence of efficacy or safety. Yet an FDA panel will consider easing access to them later this month.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: After Trump's reelection, these U.S. scientists found jobs in the U.K.
candidates.description: More U.S. scientists are heading abroad. Three researchers explain why they decided to shift their research to universities in the U.K.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Sing-along time at a memory cafe is a highlight for people dealing with dementia
candidates.description: Music tends to stay with people even as dementia claims other memories. In Vermont, Memory Cafes provide communities for both dementia patients and their caregivers.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: They were world-class tennis rivals. Now friends, they've teamed up against cancer
candidates.description: They were the women's tennis champions of their generation. Now, Chris Evert and Martina Navratilova open up about friendship, cancer and retirement in the documentary Chris & Martina: The Final Set .
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: People are taking allergy and heartburn pills for PMS. Could it work?
candidates.description: Can the mix really help with premenstrual woes? Theoretically, the link between histamine and hormones could explain why the trendy DIY treatment seems to help some women.
candidates.author: NPR
